reportMeta.title: Video Impact Analysis — Pre vs. Post InPageDiscovery

runA.label: Run A — Pre-InPageDiscovery
runA.note: Run A's demo-package.json was overwritten by Run B. Data reconstructed from discovery-coverage-report.json, voice-script.json, and session analysis.

runB.label: Run B — Post-InPageDiscovery
runB.note: Run B wrote a new demo-package.json but the video was NOT re-rendered. The demo-video-with-voice.mp4 on disk is from Run A. Run B's output exists only as a JSON manifest.

criticalFindings.title: Run B video was never rendered
criticalFindings.detail: Run B's pipeline completed at 12:34. The demo-video-with-voice.mp4 on disk was last written at 11:25 — 69 minutes BEFORE Run B ran. The current physical video is from Run A. Every comparison below is between Run A's actual rendered video and Run B's unrendered demo-package.json manifest.
criticalFindings.impact: All InPageDiscovery improvements in Run B are invisible in the current video output. The rendered video does not reflect any post-integration work.

criticalFindings.title: Journey Generator routed through the login page twice
criticalFindings.detail: Run B's journey places pages 2ac6c474 (/) and f39ec4fc (/) as scenes 9 and 10. Both are the root/login screen with 0 interaction targets, 3 base features (lowest in the pipeline), and visually identical screenshots (same SHA-256 hash). These two scenes consume 37.5 seconds (39%) of the 90-second main content window.
criticalFindings.impact: 17% of total video runtime wasted on a login screen that has no feature to demonstrate. This is the single largest quality regression from Run A to Run B.

criticalFindings.title: 55% of Run B storyboard scenes are generic fallback
criticalFindings.detail: 6 of 11 scenes in Run B have title='Rheem TotalView' and narration='Rheem TotalView: manage Rheem TotalView...' — the StoryboardGenerator's fallback template when no specific ranked feature is mapped to the scene's page. This despite features #6 (Real-Time Alert Feed) and #8-9 (Device Monitoring, Device Health) being in the top-10 and directly corresponding to the pages of those scenes.
criticalFindings.impact: Scenes 6–11 produce uninformative, repetitive narration that cannot be used in a customer-facing video. Run A had 0 generic fallback scenes.

criticalFindings.title: No interaction state screenshots appear in any scene
criticalFindings.detail: All 11 Run B scenes use captures/{pageId}/viewport.png (base screenshots). Zero scenes use interactions/ screenshots. The 9 meaningful interaction states discovered by InPageDiscovery are completely invisible to the video renderer. The INVARIANT (states never enter ctx.pageCaptures) means Remotion will never see them under the current architecture.
criticalFindings.impact: The richer visual content discovered via interaction (expanded panels, opened drawers, active configuration states) is unreachable by the video layer. Users only ever see the static base view.

criticalFindings.title: Voice narration mismatches Run B scene content
criticalFindings.detail: voice-script.json (written at 11:07, before Run B) describes Run A's journey. Scene 5 voice says 'Device Status & Connectivity' but Run B scene 5 shows /simulator with 'Scenario Playback Speed'. Scene 9 voice says 'The built-in simulator lets you test failure scenarios' but Run B scene 9 shows the login page. The voice-script.json has never been updated to reflect Run B.
criticalFindings.impact: If Run B were rendered, the audio narration would be completely wrong for scenes 5–11.

delta.note: InPageDiscovery's +40 features strengthened high-scoring dashboard/energy metrics (already well-represented) and pushed 5 specific page-level features out of the top-10. The one net gain was Scenario Playback Speed (simulator only).

journeyPath.ANOMALY: DUPLICATE — same URL and same screenshot as scene-9

delta.loginPageFramesWasted: 540 frames (18 seconds at 30fps) — scenes 9+10 each 270 frames
delta.rootCause: JourneyGenerator's TopN supplement fills missing steps by appending discovered pages in order. Root (/) appears twice in ctx.discoveredPages because BFS reached it via two different crawl paths. The supplement does not deduplicate by URL, only by pageId — and both occurrences have distinct pageIds (2ac6c474 and f39ec4fc).
delta.recommendation: JourneyGenerator TopN supplement must deduplicate by URL before appending pages. Login/unauthenticated pages should be excluded from supplement candidates (heuristic: pages with 0 interaction targets or 0 ranked features).

runB.note: Run B's demo-package.json was written at 12:34, 69 minutes after the physical video was last rendered. No re-render has been performed. All Run B analysis is from the unrendered manifest only.

screenshotsInVideo.note: Same screenshot source as Run A. InPageDiscovery state screenshots are inaccessible to renderer by design.

narration.source: demo-package.json auto-generated (voice-script.json not updated for Run B)
mismatchDetails: Scene 5 auto-narration says 'Scenario Playback Speed' but current voice-script.json says 'Device Status'
Scene 9 voice says 'the built-in simulator' but scene shows login page

detail.note: ★ 'Scenario Playback Speed' derived from state vision analysis
State features merged into existing ranked features; no new scene content
'Add Building Action' was in Run A features; state reinforced existing feature, no net gain
'Real-Time Alert Feed' reached top-10 but Storyboard didn't assign it to the alarms scene
'Device Health' reached top-10 but Storyboard assigned it as secondary in scene-1 description only
Energy & Cost features reinforced but already covered in scene-2
No settings-specific feature entered top-10; scene-11 is generic fallback
No AI-specific feature entered top-10; scene-8 is generic fallback

primaryBottleneck.specificIssue: TopN supplement does not deduplicate by URL or filter out zero-value pages
evidence: Root/login page (/) appears twice in Run B journey (scenes 9 and 10)
Both root page instances have 0 interaction targets, 3 features (lowest in pipeline), and visually identical base screenshots (same SHA-256 hash)
These two scenes consume 540 frames (18 seconds) = 20% of the 90-second main content window
Run A had 0 login page occurrences in its journey
primaryBottleneck.fix: JourneyGenerator.TopNSupplement must: (1) deduplicate candidate pages by URL before selection, (2) exclude pages with featureCount < threshold or interactionTargets == 0 from supplement pool

secondaryBottleneck.specificIssue: Feature-to-page assignment fails for 6 of 11 scenes despite ranked features covering those pages
evidence: Scene 6 (alarms page): 'Real-Time Alert Feed' is ranked #6 globally but not assigned → generic fallback
Scene 7 (devices page): 'Device Health & Offline Tracking' is ranked #9 but assigned as secondary in scene-1, not scene-7
Scene 8 (ai page): No AI-specific feature reached top-10 — InPageDiscovery found 4 features on /ai state but none broke into ranked output
Scenes 9-10 (login page): No feature exists for login page — downstream of Journey bottleneck
secondaryBottleneck.fix: StoryboardGenerator must: (1) assign each ranked feature to its SOURCE page's scene when that page appears in the journey, (2) prevent a feature from being used as a secondary mention in scene X when its primary page has its own scene in the journey

tertiaryBottleneck.specificIssue: 88.9% deduplication rate collapsed 90 → 10. Specific page-level features (Failure Prediction Cards, Team Assignment) were outranked by broader dashboard metrics boosted by state analysis
evidence: Run A had 'Failure Prediction Cards' as a ranked feature → scene-4 was specific and informative
Run B top-10 has no AI/prediction-specific feature → /ai page (scene-8) is generic fallback
Run A had 'Team Assignment & Tagging' as a ranked feature → scene-3 was specific
Run B replaces it with broader 'Structured User List Table' which is less differentiated
tertiaryBottleneck.fix: Ensure at least one unique feature per discovered page enters the ranked output (page-diversity constraint). Consider increasing topN from 10 to 15-20 to reduce the tail cutoff.

quaternaryBottleneck.specificIssue: Interaction state screenshots are architecturally blocked from reaching the video renderer
evidence: All 11 Run B scenes use captures/{pageId}/viewport.png (base screenshots)
0 scenes use interactions/ screenshots
9 meaningful interaction states with richer visual content are invisible to Remotion
Scenario Playback Speed scene (scene-5) shows the static /simulator base screenshot, not the interaction state that revealed the playback controls
quaternaryBottleneck.fix: StoryboardScene schema needs a new optional field: interactionStatePath. When a scene's feature was discovered exclusively via interaction state, the storyboard should reference the state screenshot rather than the base viewport. Requires coordination between InPageDiscoveryStage (which knows which states are meaningful) and StoryboardGenerator.
quaternaryBottleneck.architecturalNote: The current INVARIANT (states never enter ctx.pageCaptures) is correct and should be preserved. The fix is to pass interactionStatePaths from PageExplorationSummary into StoryboardGenerator's scene assembly, not to break the base/state separation.

notABottleneck.reason: All 11 BFS-reachable pages appear in both runs. No pages are being lost at this stage.
All 11 base pages and all 9 interaction states were analyzed in vision mode. No analysis failures.

The stage worked correctly. 17 targets explored, 9 meaningful states found, 9 state vision analyses completed, 1 unique feature reached the final video. The bottleneck is downstream (ranking + storyboard + rendering), not within the discovery stage.

videoNotRerenderedBottleneck.specificIssue: Run B's demo-package.json was never rendered into a physical video
evidence: demo-package.json last written: 12-06-2026 12:34:13
demo-video.mp4 last written: 12-06-2026 11:16:52 (69 minutes earlier)
demo-video-with-voice.mp4 last written: 12-06-2026 11:25:25 (69 minutes earlier)
The current physical video on disk is from Run A's pipeline output
videoNotRerenderedBottleneck.fix: Run `npm run render:demo` (or equivalent) to produce demo-video.mp4 from Run B's demo-package.json. Then update voice-script.json to reflect Run B's scene ordering and re-run `npm run voice` to regenerate narration.

prioritisedActionPlan.action: Fix JourneyGenerator TopN supplement: deduplicate by URL + filter zero-value pages
prioritisedActionPlan.expectedImpact: Removes 2 login page scenes (180+ frames freed), allowing /alarms, /devices, /ai to get properly utilized scenes. Projected improvement: 9-11 specific scenes vs current 5.
prioritisedActionPlan.effort: Low — single filter condition in supplement logic

prioritisedActionPlan.action: Fix StoryboardGenerator feature-to-page assignment: each ranked feature must bind to its source page's scene
prioritisedActionPlan.expectedImpact: Eliminates generic fallback for /alarms (Real-Time Alert Feed), /devices (Device Health), and potentially /settings. Projected improvement: 8-9 specific scenes.
prioritisedActionPlan.effort: Medium — requires feature→pageId mapping to flow through to storyboard scene builder

prioritisedActionPlan.action: Re-render video from Run B demo-package.json
prioritisedActionPlan.expectedImpact: Produces a video that actually reflects Run B's improvements (including Scenario Playback Speed scene). Currently the on-disk video shows no InPageDiscovery benefit at all.

prioritisedActionPlan.action: Update voice-script.json for Run B scene ordering, regenerate TTS
prioritisedActionPlan.expectedImpact: Aligns audio narration with the actual scenes in Run B. Scene 5 (Scenario Playback Speed) and scene ordering must be re-described.
prioritisedActionPlan.effort: Low — manual edit of voice-script.json then `npm run voice`

prioritisedActionPlan.action: Increase feature topN from 10 to 15-20, add page-diversity constraint
prioritisedActionPlan.expectedImpact: Restores specific features for /ai and /settings that were pushed out of the top-10. Prevents broad dashboard features from crowding out page-specific features.

prioritisedActionPlan.action: Pass interactionStatePaths into StoryboardGenerator scene assembly
prioritisedActionPlan.expectedImpact: Enables scenes to show the richer interaction state screenshot instead of static base view. Scenario Playback Speed scene would show the actual playback controls UI discovered via InPageDiscovery, not the static simulator landing screen.
prioritisedActionPlan.effort: Medium — requires schema change in StoryboardScene + assembly logic
